tavily.raw: {"query":"New Caledonia provincial elections rescheduled new date 2026","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.rnz.co.nz/news/pacific_new-caledonia/596736/french-constitutional-council-approves-changes-to-new-caledonia-s-electoral-roll","title":"French Constitutional Council approves changes to New Caledonia's electoral roll | RNZ","content":"# French Constitutional Council approves changes to New Caledonia's electoral roll. New Caledonia’s special electoral card for Congress and provincial elections. The French Constitutional Council on Thursday validated an adjustment to New Caledonia's restrictions for their forthcoming provincial elections due to be held on 28 June 2026. The ruling from the French body comes at the request of the French Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu, after the amendments, in the form of an \"organic law\", were endorsed by both the National Assembly and the Senate. The partial \"unfreezing\" of New Caledonia's electoral roll mainly targets New Caledonia's population who were born after restrictions were imposed as part of the implementation of the Nouméa Accord signed in 1998 and the organic law of 19 March 1999. New Caledonia’s MP at the French National Assembly Emmanuel Tjibaou after French National Assembly endorsed changes in New Caledonia’s electoral roll for provincial elections on 20 May 2026.","score":0.8755427,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_New_Caledonian_legislative_election","title":"2026 New Caledonian legislative election","content":"Legislative elections were held in New Caledonia on 28 June 2026 to elect members of the Congress of New Caledonia. The results of the election, initially planned for 2024, will determine the balance of power in New Caledonia ahead of fresh negotiations with France on the territory's status, with independence remaining the defining political issue.
